q1_coverage_gaps: server/upload.go: StartUpload / blobUpload.Run — upload abort path (if Run fails mid-upload, are partial files cleaned up? no partial-state cleanup observed)
manifest/manifest.go: Manifests() and ParseNamedManifest() — no coverage yet on manifest parsing size caps
server/internal/client/ollama/registry.go: chunksums stream — no hypothesis covers the chunksum URL injection or chunksum integrity
q2_chain_seeds: PH-01/15 (path traversal write) chains into: PH-08/16 (file read via push), PH-02/20 (model substitution after planting correct-size file), and PH-07 (fixBlobs rename)
PH-03/17 (SSRF) chains into PH-04 + PH-18 (OOM via large response from SSRF target)
PH-01 + PH-10 (empty file creation) chains into authenticated file write at any traversal path
q3_fragile_items: PH-06 (slow-retry goroutine race) — Fragile; requires goroutine-cancellation race win
PH-07 (fixBlobs symlink rename) — Fragile; requires prior write access
PH-09/19 (realm HTTP downgrade) — Conditional; requires MITM or DNS control of registry host
q4_model_coverage_gaps: server/internal/client/ollama/registry.go:chunksums — chunksum URL is registry-controlled (attacker can supply CDN URL for each chunk); chunk digest validation needs verification
server/upload.go upload abort cleanup path — not covered by either reasoner
manifest/manifest.go manifest parsing size cap — manifest JSON body fed to json.Unmarshal without size limit verified only for pullModelManifest; Registry.Resolve also has io.ReadAll without limit
q5_impact_multipliers: PH-07 (fixBlobs symlink rename): if validated, chains with PH-01 to enable rename of any file on the system
PH-13 (imagegen BlobPath traversal): if confirmed as production-reachable, doubles the path-traversal write surface
loops.decision_rationale: All entry points from attack surface map are covered. Fragile items (PH-06, PH-07, PH-09) have been characterized with their conditions. Q4 gaps (chunksums, upload abort) have lower risk signal than already-validated findings. Q3 fragile items do not have new unexplored attack angles. Proceeding to summary after documenting the two NEEDS-DEEPER items.
